description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to produce a very small (but precise) certificate-failure summary for your team.

    Current situation
    • A single log file already exists at /home/user/logs/ssl_error.log  
      – You do NOT need to create this file; it is already populated.  

    Goal
    1. Make a new directory called /home/user/cert_debug  
    2. Inside that directory create a file named cert_error_summary.log that contains an aggregated view of certificate-verification failures extracted from /home/user/logs/ssl_error.log.  

    Exact verification requirements for /home/user/cert_debug/cert_error_summary.log
    • The file must be plain text.  
    • The first N lines must list every unique failed-certificate domain and the number of times it appears in the log, formatted as:  

          <domain>,<count>

      (No spaces before or after the comma.)  
    • Sort those lines alphabetically by the domain name (simple lexicographic sort).  
    • After that list, append a single line that shows the overall time range observed in the log, formatted exactly as:  

          TimeRange: <earliest_timestamp_ISO> - <latest_timestamp_ISO>

      where each ISO timestamp is in the form YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS (note the literal “T” between date and time).  
    • End the file with a newline.  

    Example of the required final layout (your actual domain names and counts must come from the real log data):

        api.example.com,2
        example.com,2
        test.site,1
        TimeRange: 2024-02-01T10:15:20 - 2024-02-01T13:00:00

    Constraints
    • You should be able to accomplish this with roughly three or four shell commands (e.g., mkdir, grep/awk, sort, redirect).  
    • Do NOT modify /home/user/logs/ssl_error.log in any way.  
    • Make sure /home/user/cert_debug/cert_error_summary.log has the exact contents and order described above so that automated tests can verify it.
truth:
Initial files already present before the task starts:
    └── /home/user/logs/ssl_error.log

    /home/user/logs/ssl_error.log exact contents (each line ends with a newline):

    [2024-02-01 10:15:20] ERROR tls: certificate verify failed for domain example.com
    [2024-02-01 10:20:50] ERROR tls: certificate verify failed for domain api.example.com
    [2024-02-01 11:00:05] ERROR tls: certificate verify failed for domain example.com
    [2024-02-01 11:30:00] INFO handshake succeeded for domain good.com
    [2024-02-01 12:45:35] ERROR tls: certificate verify failed for domain test.site
    [2024-02-01 13:00:00] ERROR tls: certificate verify failed for domain api.example.com

    Required artefacts after the agent finishes:

    1. Directory: /home/user/cert_debug (normal user ownership, default 755 permissions are fine).

    2. File: /home/user/cert_debug/cert_error_summary.log  
       Exact contents (each line terminated with a single newline, last line included):

       api.example.com,2
       example.com,2
       test.site,1
       TimeRange: 2024-02-01T10:15:20 - 2024-02-01T13:00:00
name: task_000000_cc80df3f
